tasks.title: Setup Template & Dependencies
tasks.description: Set up the project template and install all required dependencies for the TraffBoardLite MVP.
tasks.details:
1. Clone the starter template from https://github.com/arhamkhnz/next-shadcn-admin-dashboard
2. Clean up the repository by removing unnecessary pages and components
3. Install and configure the following dependencies:
   - TanStack Query for data fetching
   - Zustand for state management
   - Drizzle ORM for database operations
   - PapaParse for CSV parsing
   - NextAuth.js for authentication
   - shadcn/ui and Tailwind CSS for UI components
   - Zod for validation
4. Set up TypeScript configuration
5. Create basic folder structure following Next.js 15 App Router conventions
6. Configure ESLint and Prettier for code quality
7. Set up environment variables structure (.env.example)
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Verify all dependencies are correctly installed by checking package.json
2. Ensure the application builds without errors using 'npm run build'
3. Confirm the development server starts correctly with 'npm run dev'
4. Validate TypeScript configuration by checking for type errors
5. Test basic navigation to ensure the template is working correctly

subtasks.title: Клонировать шаблон next-shadcn-admin-dashboard
subtasks.description: Склонировать репозиторий https://github.com/arhamkhnz/next-shadcn-admin-dashboard как основу для проекта.
subtasks.details: Выполнить git clone, убедиться, что проект запускается локально.

subtasks.title: Clean up the repository
subtasks.description: Remove unnecessary pages and components from the starter template to keep only the essentials.
subtasks.details: Delete demo pages, unused components, and example content. Ensure the codebase is minimal and ready for customization.

subtasks.title: Install and configure dependencies
subtasks.description: Install and set up all required libraries for the MVP.
subtasks.details: Install TanStack Query, Zustand, Drizzle ORM, PapaParse, NextAuth.js, shadcn/ui, Tailwind CSS, and Zod. Ensure all dependencies are properly configured and working.

subtasks.title: Set up TypeScript configuration
subtasks.details: Adjust tsconfig.json and related files for optimal type safety and compatibility with Next.js 15.

subtasks.title: Create basic folder structure
subtasks.description: Organize the project folders following Next.js 15 App Router conventions.
subtasks.details: Set up folders for app, components, hooks, utils, config, and other essentials.

subtasks.title: Configure ESLint and Prettier
subtasks.description: Set up code quality tools for the project.
subtasks.details: Add and configure ESLint and Prettier for consistent code style and linting.

subtasks.title: Set up environment variables structure
subtasks.description: Prepare .env.example and document required environment variables.
subtasks.details: List all necessary environment variables for local and production environments. Create .env.example file.

tasks.title: Database Setup & Models
tasks.description: Configure the database connection and create the required data models using Drizzle ORM with PostgreSQL.
tasks.details:
1. Set up Drizzle ORM configuration for PostgreSQL connection
2. Create database schema files for the following models:
   - users table with fields: id, email, passwordHash, role, totpSecret, createdAt
   - conversions table with fields: id, date, partnerId, campaignId, clicks, registrations, revenue, createdAt
3. Implement the exact schema as defined in the PRD:
```typescript
export const users = pgTable('users', {
  id: serial('id').primaryKey(),
  email: varchar('email', { length: 255 }).unique().notNull(),
  passwordHash: text('password_hash').notNull(),
  role: varchar('role', { length: 50 }).default('user').notNull(),
  totpSecret: text('totp_secret'),
  createdAt: timestamp('created_at').defaultNow().notNull(),
})

export const conversions = pgTable('conversions', {
  id: serial('id').primaryKey(),
  date: date('date').notNull(),
  partnerId: varchar('partner_id', { length: 100 }).notNull(),
  campaignId: varchar('campaign_id', { length: 100 }).notNull(),
  clicks: integer('clicks').default(0).notNull(),
  registrations: integer('registrations').default(0).notNull(),
  revenue: decimal('revenue', { precision: 10, scale: 2 }).default('0').notNull(),
  createdAt: timestamp('created_at').defaultNow().notNull(),
})
```
4. Set up drizzle-kit for database migrations
5. Create seed data script for testing with sample users and conversions
6. Implement database utility functions for common operations
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Test database connection using a simple query
2. Verify migrations run successfully
3. Confirm seed data is correctly inserted
4. Test CRUD operations on both models
5. Validate constraints (e.g., unique email, not null fields)
6. Ensure proper error handling for database operations

tasks.title: Authentication & 2FA
tasks.description: Implement user authentication with NextAuth.js including 2FA support using TOTP and role-based access control.
tasks.details:
1. Configure NextAuth.js with the following:
   - Custom Drizzle adapter for user storage
   - Credentials provider for email/password login
   - Session handling and JWT configuration
2. Implement TOTP (Time-based One-Time Password) 2FA:
   - Generate and store TOTP secrets in the user model
   - Create QR code generation for initial setup
   - Implement TOTP verification during login
3. Create protected route middleware:
   - Check for authenticated sessions
   - Implement role-based access control (user/superuser)
   - Redirect unauthenticated users to login
4. Build authentication UI components:
   - Login form with email/password
   - 2FA verification input
   - 2FA setup flow for new users
5. Implement logout functionality
6. Add session persistence and refresh logic
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Test user registration and login flows
2. Verify 2FA setup and verification works correctly
3. Test protected routes with authenticated and unauthenticated users
4. Confirm role-based access control functions properly
5. Validate session persistence across page refreshes
6. Test error handling for invalid credentials
7. Verify logout functionality clears session data

tasks.title: Basic Layout & Navigation
tasks.description: Create the application layout with responsive design and role-based navigation using shadcn/ui components.
tasks.details:
1. Update the sidebar navigation from the template:
   - Dashboard link (all users)
   - Upload link (superusers only)
   - Users management link (superusers only)
2. Create page structure following Next.js App Router:
   - app/dashboard/page.tsx
   - app/upload/page.tsx
   - app/users/page.tsx
3. Implement responsive layout with shadcn/ui:
   - Mobile-friendly sidebar with collapse/expand
   - Header with user profile dropdown
   - Main content area with proper padding
4. Add role-based menu visibility:
   - Use session data to determine user role
   - Conditionally render navigation items based on role
   - Handle unauthorized access attempts
5. Create loading and error states for all pages
6. Implement theme support (light/dark mode)
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Test navigation between all pages
2. Verify role-based menu items display correctly for different user types
3. Test responsive design on various screen sizes
4. Confirm unauthorized users cannot access restricted pages
5. Validate loading and error states display correctly
6. Test theme switching functionality

tasks.title: CSV Upload & Processing
tasks.description: Implement CSV file upload functionality with validation and processing to store conversion data in the database.
tasks.details:
1. Create file upload form using shadcn/ui components:
   - Drag and drop area
   - File selection button
   - Upload progress indicator
2. Implement server action for file processing:
   - Use PapaParse to parse CSV data
   - Apply Zod schema validation to ensure data integrity
   - Map CSV columns to conversion model fields
3. Add validation rules for CSV structure:
   - Required columns: date, partnerId, campaignId, clicks, registrations, revenue
   - Data type validation for each field
   - Date format validation
4. Implement database operations:
   - Batch insert processed records into conversions table
   - Handle duplicate entries
   - Transaction support for atomicity
5. Create error handling and reporting:
   - Display validation errors to user
   - Show summary of processed records
   - Allow retrying failed uploads
6. Add superuser-only access control to the upload functionality
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Test file upload with valid CSV files
2. Verify validation catches malformed CSV data
3. Test processing of large files (performance test)
4. Confirm data is correctly inserted into the database
5. Validate error handling for various failure scenarios
6. Test access control to ensure only superusers can upload
7. Verify the upload summary displays accurate information

tasks.title: Analytics Dashboard
tasks.description: Develop the analytics dashboard with filters, KPI cards, and charts to visualize conversion data.
tasks.details:
1. Implement global filter components using Zustand:
```typescript
interface FilterState {
  dateRange: [Date, Date]
  partners: string[]
  campaigns: string[]
  setFilters: (filters: Partial<FilterState>) => void
  resetFilters: () => void
}

export const useFilterStore = create<FilterState>((set) => ({
  dateRange: [startOfMonth(new Date()), new Date()],
  partners: [],
  campaigns: [],
  setFilters: (filters) => set((state) => ({ ...state, ...filters })),
  resetFilters: () => set({
    dateRange: [startOfMonth(new Date()), new Date()],
    partners: [],
    campaigns: []
  })
}))
```
2. Create data fetching with TanStack Query:
```typescript
export const useAnalytics = () => {
  const filters = useFilterStore()
  
  return useQuery({
    queryKey: ['analytics', filters],
    queryFn: () => fetchAnalytics(filters),
    staleTime: 5 * 60 * 1000, // 5 minutes
  })
}
```
3. Implement filter UI components:
   - Date range picker
   - Partner multi-select dropdown
   - Campaign multi-select dropdown
   - Reset filters button
4. Create KPI cards for key metrics:
   - Total clicks
   - Total registrations
   - Total revenue
   - Conversion rate
5. Implement charts using shadcn/ui charts (Recharts wrapper):
   - Line chart for trends over time
   - Bar chart for partner comparison
   - Pie chart for campaign distribution
6. Add responsive design for dashboard components
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Test filter functionality with various combinations
2. Verify data fetching with TanStack Query works correctly
3. Confirm charts render properly with different data sets
4. Test responsive design on various screen sizes
5. Validate KPI calculations are accurate
6. Test performance with large data sets
7. Verify filter state persists across page refreshes

tasks.title: User Management
tasks.description: Create user management interface for superusers to create, read, update, and delete users with role assignment.
tasks.details:
1. Implement user list view with data table:
   - Display user email, role, and creation date
   - Add sorting and pagination
   - Include search functionality
2. Create user forms:
   - New user creation form with email, password, and role
   - Edit user form for updating existing users
   - Delete confirmation dialog
3. Implement role management:
   - Role selection dropdown (user/superuser)
   - Role-based permission explanation
4. Add 2FA management:
   - Reset 2FA option for administrators
   - 2FA status indicator
5. Implement server actions for user operations:
   - Create user with password hashing
   - Update user details
   - Delete user with confirmation
6. Add superuser-only access control to all user management features
7. Implement optimistic updates for better UX
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Test CRUD operations for user management
2. Verify role assignment works correctly
3. Test search, sort, and pagination functionality
4. Confirm access control restricts user management to superusers only
5. Validate form validation for all user forms
6. Test error handling for various failure scenarios
7. Verify optimistic updates provide good user experience

tasks.title: Deploy to App Platform
tasks.description: Configure and deploy the application to DigitalOcean App Platform with managed PostgreSQL database.
tasks.details:
1. Create app.yaml configuration file as specified in the PRD:
```yaml
name: traffboard-mvp
services:
- name: web
  source_dir: /
  github:
    repo: your-username/traffboard-mvp
    branch: main
  build_command: npm run build
  run_command: npm start
  environment_slug: node-js
  instance_count: 1
  instance_size_slug: basic-xxs
  envs:
  - key: DATABASE_URL
    scope: RUN_TIME
    type: SECRET
  - key: NEXTAUTH_SECRET
    scope: RUN_TIME
    type: SECRET
  - key: NEXTAUTH_URL
    scope: RUN_TIME
    value: ${APP_URL}

databases:
- engine: PG
  name: traffboard-db
  num_nodes: 1
  size: db-s-1vcpu-1gb
```
2. Set up DigitalOcean Managed PostgreSQL:
   - Create database instance
   - Configure connection settings
   - Set up firewall rules
3. Configure environment variables:
   - Database connection string
   - NextAuth secret
   - Other application secrets
4. Run database migrations on deployment:
   - Create migration script
   - Add to build or post-deploy process
5. Set up domain and HTTPS:
   - Configure custom domain
   - Enable automatic HTTPS
6. Implement CI/CD pipeline:
   - Configure auto-deploy on git push
   - Add build checks
7. Create monitoring and logging setup
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Test deployment process end-to-end
2. Verify database connection works in production
3. Confirm migrations run successfully
4. Test application functionality in production environment
5. Validate HTTPS is working correctly
6. Check environment variables are properly set
7. Test CI/CD pipeline with a sample change
8. Verify performance meets targets (page load <3s, CSV processing <60s for 50K records, filter updates <1s)
